questions.questionText: In Power BI data modeling, what is the recommended schema design for optimal performance?
options: A. Flat table with all columns.
B. Star schema with fact and dimension tables.
C. Fully normalized third normal form.
D. Network/graph model.
questions.explanation: A star schema with fact tables (measures/events) and dimension tables (descriptive attributes) is the recommended design for Power BI. It provides optimal query performance and simplifies DAX calculations. Flat tables lead to redundancy. Fully normalized schemas cause complex joins. Network models are not supported.

questions.questionText: What does the CALCULATE function do in DAX?
options: A. Creates a new calculated column.
B. Evaluates an expression in a modified filter context.
C. Imports data from an external source.
D. Formats numbers as currency.
questions.explanation: CALCULATE evaluates an expression in a modified filter context, allowing you to override or add filters to change how an expression is evaluated. It does not create columns, import data, or format numbers.

questions.questionText: In Power Query, which step would you use to convert column data types from text to whole number?
options: A. Split Column.
B. Change Type.
C. Merge Columns.
D. Unpivot Columns.
questions.explanation: Change Type is the Power Query step that converts column data types (e.g., text to whole number, date to text). Split Column divides text into multiple columns. Merge Columns combines columns. Unpivot reshapes columns into rows.

questions.questionText: Which visualization type is most appropriate for showing the proportion of each category relative to the total?
options: A. Line chart.
B. Scatter plot.
C. Pie chart or donut chart.
D. Waterfall chart.
questions.explanation: Pie charts and donut charts show parts of a whole, making them ideal for displaying category proportions. Line charts show trends over time. Scatter plots show correlations between two measures. Waterfall charts show cumulative effects of sequential values.

questions.questionText: How do you implement row-level security (RLS) in Power BI to restrict data access based on the user viewing the report?
options: A. Create separate reports for each user.
B. Define roles with DAX filter expressions in the model and assign users to roles in the Power BI service.
C. Use Power Query parameters for each user.
D. Create different workspaces for each department.
questions.explanation: RLS is implemented by defining roles with DAX filter expressions in Power BI Desktop and then assigning users or groups to those roles in the Power BI service. Creating separate reports is not scalable. Power Query parameters are for data transformation. Separate workspaces do not provide row-level filtering.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of deployment pipelines in Power BI?
options: A. To schedule data refresh.
B. To manage the lifecycle of Power BI content across Development, Test, and Production stages.
C. To create data alerts.
D. To export reports to PDF.
questions.explanation: Deployment pipelines provide a structured way to manage Power BI content lifecycle across Development, Test, and Production stages, enabling controlled promotion of content. Data refresh scheduling, alerts, and PDF export are separate features.

questions.questionText: In the Power BI service, what is a workspace used for?
options: A. Storing personal bookmarks.
B. Collaborating on and managing collections of dashboards, reports, datasets, and dataflows.
C. Configuring on-premises gateway connections only.
D. Managing Power BI Desktop installations.
questions.explanation: Workspaces are collaborative environments where teams can create, manage, and share dashboards, reports, datasets, and dataflows. Personal bookmarks are user-specific. Gateway connections are configured within workspaces but are not the workspace's primary purpose. Desktop installations are managed locally.

questions.questionText: What is the primary benefit of using Power BI dataflows?
options: A. To create real-time streaming dashboards.
B. To define reusable data preparation and transformation logic that can be shared across multiple datasets.
C. To replace Power Query in Power BI Desktop.
D. To host SQL Server databases.
questions.explanation: Dataflows enable centralized, reusable data preparation logic using Power Query Online that multiple datasets can reference, promoting consistency and reducing duplication. They are not for real-time streaming, do not replace Power Query in Desktop, and are not SQL Server hosts.

questions.questionText: Which DAX function calculates the year-to-date total of a measure?
options: A. TOTALYTD.
B. DATEADD.
C. FIRSTDATE.
D. CALENDAR.
questions.explanation: TOTALYTD evaluates an expression from the beginning of the year to the current date in context, providing a year-to-date calculation. DATEADD shifts dates by an interval. FIRSTDATE returns the first date in a context. CALENDAR generates a date table.

questions.questionText: In Power Query, what does merging two queries accomplish?
options: A. It appends rows from one query to another.
B. It performs a join between two queries based on matching columns, similar to a SQL JOIN.
C. It deletes duplicate rows.
D. It creates a new parameter.
questions.explanation: Merging queries in Power Query performs a join operation (Left Outer, Right Outer, Full Outer, Inner, Left Anti, Right Anti) between two tables based on matching key columns. Appending stacks rows. Removing duplicates is a separate step. Parameters are created separately.

questions.questionText: What is the default relationship cardinality when Power BI automatically detects a relationship between two tables?
options: A. Many-to-many.
B. One-to-one.
C. Many-to-one (or one-to-many).
D. No relationship is created.
questions.explanation: Power BI's auto-detect feature typically creates many-to-one (or one-to-many) relationships based on matching column names and data. Many-to-many requires explicit configuration. One-to-one is less common. Auto-detect does create relationships when it finds matching columns.

questions.questionText: What is the key difference between a DAX measure and a calculated column?
options: A. Measures are evaluated at query time based on filter context; calculated columns are computed row by row during data refresh.
B. Measures and calculated columns are identical.
C. Calculated columns are faster than measures.
D. Measures can only be used in tables, not visuals.
questions.explanation: Measures are evaluated dynamically at query time based on the current filter context and do not consume storage. Calculated columns are computed row by row during data refresh and stored in the model. They serve different purposes. Calculated columns are not inherently faster. Measures are primarily used in visuals.

questions.questionText: How can you highlight cells in a Power BI table or matrix visual based on their values?
options: A. By using a slicer.
B. By applying conditional formatting rules (background color, font color, data bars, or icons).
C. By creating a separate visual for each value.
D. By adding a page filter.
questions.explanation: Conditional formatting allows you to apply background colors, font colors, data bars, or icons to table and matrix cells based on their values or rules. Slicers filter data. Separate visuals do not highlight cells. Page filters affect all visuals, not formatting.

questions.questionText: Which DAX function is commonly used in dynamic row-level security to filter data based on the logged-in user's email address?
options: A. CALCULATE.
B. USERPRINCIPALNAME().
C. SUMMARIZE.
D. RANKX.
questions.explanation: USERPRINCIPALNAME() returns the email address (UPN) of the current user, which can be used in a DAX filter expression to dynamically restrict data to rows matching that user. CALCULATE modifies filter context. SUMMARIZE creates summary tables. RANKX calculates rankings.

questions.questionText: In Power Query, what happens when you delete a step from the Applied Steps pane?
options: A. All subsequent steps are also removed.
B. The transformation defined by that step is removed, and subsequent steps may produce errors if they depend on it.
C. Nothing changes; steps cannot be deleted.
D. The data source is disconnected.
questions.explanation: Deleting a step removes that transformation, and any subsequent steps that reference columns or data produced by the deleted step may error. Not all subsequent steps are necessarily removed. Steps can be deleted. The data source connection is not affected.

questions.questionText: Why is a dedicated date table required for DAX time intelligence functions to work correctly?
options: A. Power BI cannot store dates without a date table.
B. Time intelligence functions require a contiguous date column marked as a date table.
C. Date tables improve visual rendering speed.
D. Date tables are only needed for real-time dashboards.
questions.explanation: DAX time intelligence functions (TOTALYTD, SAMEPERIODLASTYEAR, etc.) require a contiguous date column in a table marked as a date table to correctly calculate time-based comparisons. Power BI can store dates anywhere. Date tables do not primarily improve rendering speed. They are needed for any time intelligence, not just real-time.

questions.questionText: What does the FILTER function return in DAX?
options: A. A scalar value.
B. A table that is a subset of the input table, containing only rows that meet the specified condition.
C. A formatted text string.
D. A Boolean true/false value.
questions.explanation: FILTER is a table function that iterates over a table and returns a subset containing only the rows where the condition evaluates to true. It does not return a scalar, text string, or Boolean value.

questions.questionText: Which two workspace roles in the Power BI service can publish content to the workspace? (Choose two.)
options: A. Admin.
B. Member.
C. Contributor.
D. Viewer.
questions.explanation: Admin and Member roles can publish content to a workspace. Contributors can also edit and publish content in the workspace. Viewers have read-only access and cannot publish. Note: Contributors can also publish, but among the choices given, Admin and Member are the two listed options that can publish.

questions.questionText: Which Power Query function is used to connect to a SQL Server database?
options: A. Sql.Database.
B. Excel.Workbook.
C. Web.Contents.
D. Csv.Document.
questions.explanation: Sql.Database is the Power Query M function used to connect to a SQL Server database. Excel.Workbook connects to Excel files. Web.Contents retrieves web content. Csv.Document reads CSV files.

questions.questionText: What is the drill-through feature in Power BI used for?
options: A. To filter all pages simultaneously.
B. To navigate from a summary visual to a detail page filtered by the selected data point.
C. To export data to Excel.
D. To create bookmarks.
questions.explanation: Drill-through allows users to right-click on a data point in a visual and navigate to a detail page that is automatically filtered to show details for that specific data point. It does not filter all pages, export to Excel, or create bookmarks.

questions.questionText: How does SUMX differ from SUM in DAX?
options: A. SUMX and SUM are identical.
B. SUMX iterates over a table and sums an expression evaluated for each row; SUM aggregates a single column.
C. SUM is faster because it iterates over rows.
D. SUMX can only be used in calculated columns.
questions.explanation: SUMX is an iterator function that evaluates an expression for each row in a table and then sums the results, allowing row-by-row calculations (like Quantity * Price). SUM simply aggregates a single column. They are not identical. SUM does not iterate. SUMX can be used in measures and calculated columns.

questions.questionText: What can deployment pipeline rules be used for in Power BI?
options: A. To change the data source connection string when deploying between stages.
B. To automatically create new reports.
C. To delete content from a stage.
D. To assign user licenses.
questions.explanation: Deployment pipeline rules allow you to configure parameter or data source rules that change values (like connection strings or server names) when content is deployed from one stage to another. They do not create reports, delete content, or manage licenses.

questions.questionText: How can you use an inactive relationship in a DAX measure?
options: A. By activating it in the model view.
B. By using the USERELATIONSHIP function within a CALCULATE expression.
C. Inactive relationships cannot be used.
D. By deleting the active relationship first.
questions.explanation: The USERELATIONSHIP function activates an inactive relationship within the context of a CALCULATE expression, allowing you to use alternate relationships for specific calculations. Inactive relationships can be used without permanently activating them or deleting the active one.

questions.questionText: What does appending queries do in Power Query?
options: A. Joins tables horizontally by matching columns.
B. Stacks rows from multiple tables vertically into a single table.
C. Removes duplicate rows.
D. Creates a parameter.
questions.explanation: Appending queries stacks rows from two or more tables vertically (union), combining them into a single table. Merging joins tables horizontally. Removing duplicates is a separate operation. Parameter creation is unrelated.

questions.questionText: What do bookmarks capture in a Power BI report?
options: A. Only the current page.
B. The current state of a report page, including filters, slicers, visual visibility, and sort order.
C. Only the data model.
D. User comments and annotations.
questions.explanation: Bookmarks capture the complete state of a report page including filters, slicers, visual visibility, spotlight, and sort order, allowing you to create interactive navigation. They capture more than just the page. They do not capture the data model alone or user comments.

questions.questionText: How can you test row-level security roles in Power BI Desktop before publishing?
options: A. You cannot test RLS in Desktop.
B. By using the 'View as' option in the Modeling tab to impersonate a role.
C. By publishing to the service first.
D. By creating a test workspace.
questions.explanation: Power BI Desktop provides a 'View as' feature in the Modeling tab that lets you view the report as if you were a member of a specific role, verifying that the DAX filters work correctly. Testing is possible in Desktop. You do not need to publish first. Test workspaces are not required.

questions.questionText: What does the ALL function do when used inside CALCULATE?
options: A. Adds a new filter.
B. Removes all filters from the specified table or columns.
C. Returns only the first row.
D. Counts all rows in a table.
questions.explanation: ALL removes all filters from the specified table or columns, effectively ignoring any slicers or filters applied to those fields. This is useful for calculating totals regardless of filter context. It does not add filters, return first rows, or count rows.

questions.questionText: What is a computed entity in a Power BI dataflow?
options: A. An entity that sources data from an external database.
B. An entity that references another entity within the same dataflow, enabling reuse of transformation logic.
C. An entity that runs Python scripts.
D. An entity stored only in memory.
questions.explanation: A computed entity references another entity in the same dataflow and applies additional transformations, enabling reuse and layered data preparation. It does not source from external databases directly (that is a standard entity). It is not limited to Python scripts or memory-only storage.

questions.questionText: When would you use a many-to-many relationship in a Power BI data model?
options: A. When both tables have unique values in the join column.
B. When neither table has unique values in the join column and a bridge table is not available.
C. Many-to-many relationships are never recommended.
D. When you want to create a one-to-one relationship.
questions.explanation: Many-to-many relationships are used when both sides of the relationship have duplicate values and a bridge table to normalize the relationship is not available. When both have unique values, it is one-to-one. Many-to-many is supported but should be used carefully. It is not the same as one-to-one.

questions.questionText: Which two Power Query operations reshape data between wide and tall formats? (Choose two.)
options: A. Pivot Column.
B. Unpivot Columns.
C. Group By.
D. Sort Rows.
questions.explanation: Pivot Column converts rows to columns (tall to wide format). Unpivot Columns converts columns to rows (wide to tall format). Group By aggregates data. Sort Rows changes the order but does not reshape the data structure.

questions.questionText: When can you use the RELATED function in a calculated column?
options: A. Only in measures.
B. When there is a relationship from the current table to the target table and you need to look up a value from the target table.
C. Only between tables with no relationship.
D. Only with date tables.
questions.explanation: RELATED follows an existing relationship from the many side to the one side to retrieve a value from a related table in a calculated column context. It is primarily used in calculated columns (not measures where RELATED context differs). It requires a relationship. It works with any table type.

questions.questionText: What three components are required to configure a KPI visual in Power BI?
options: A. Measure, date axis, and category.
B. Indicator (actual value), trend axis, and target goal.
C. X-axis, Y-axis, and legend.
D. Rows, columns, and values.
questions.explanation: A KPI visual requires an Indicator (the measure being tracked), a Trend Axis (typically a date field), and a Target Goal (the target value to compare against). The other options describe different visual types like scatter plots or matrices.

questions.questionText: What is a Power BI app and how is it related to a workspace?
options: A. An app is a packaged collection of dashboards and reports from a workspace, published for broad consumption.
B. An app is the same as a workspace.
C. An app is a mobile-only feature.
D. An app can only contain one report.
questions.explanation: A Power BI app is a packaged collection of dashboards, reports, and datasets from a workspace that is published for broad consumption by end users, providing a curated read-only experience. It is not the same as a workspace, not mobile-only, and can contain multiple items.

questions.questionText: What happens when a measure is evaluated within an iterator function like SUMX?
options: A. Nothing special; the measure evaluates normally.
B. Context transition occurs, converting the current row context into a filter context for the measure.
C. The measure is skipped.
D. An error is raised.
questions.explanation: When a measure is evaluated inside an iterator, context transition automatically converts the current row context into a filter context, allowing the measure to be evaluated correctly for each row. The measure is not skipped or errored; the behavior is different from normal evaluation.

questions.questionText: How can you handle errors in a Power Query column transformation?
options: A. Errors cannot be handled in Power Query.
B. By using the Replace Errors step or try...otherwise expressions in M code.
C. By deleting the entire column.
D. By ignoring them; they disappear on refresh.
questions.explanation: Power Query provides Replace Errors to substitute error values and try...otherwise expressions in M code for granular error handling. Errors can be handled. Deleting the column loses data. Errors do not disappear on refresh without correction.

questions.questionText: What is the purpose of aggregation tables in a Power BI data model?
options: A. To store raw transactional data.
B. To pre-aggregate data at a higher grain for faster query performance, with automatic fallback to detail tables.
C. To replace all dimension tables.
D. To export data to Excel.
questions.explanation: Aggregation tables store pre-aggregated data at a higher grain, and Power BI automatically uses them when possible, falling back to detail tables when more granularity is needed. This improves performance for large datasets. They do not store raw data, replace dimensions, or export to Excel.

questions.questionText: How can you create a custom tooltip page in Power BI that appears when hovering over data points in a visual?
options: A. By adding a text box to each visual.
B. By creating a report page with the tooltip page size setting enabled and configuring visuals to use it.
C. Custom tooltips are not supported.
D. By adding comments to the data model.
questions.explanation: You create a report page, set its page size type to Tooltip in the page formatting options, design the tooltip content, and then configure other visuals to use this page as their tooltip. Text boxes are static. Custom tooltips are supported. Data model comments do not create tooltips.

questions.questionText: What is the SWITCH function used for in DAX?
options: A. To switch between data sources.
B. To evaluate an expression against a list of values and return the corresponding result.
C. To toggle visuals on and off.
D. To switch between Import and DirectQuery modes.
questions.explanation: SWITCH evaluates an expression against a list of value-result pairs and returns the result for the first matching value. It is similar to a CASE statement in SQL. It does not switch data sources, toggle visuals, or change connection modes.

questions.questionText: Which two features does the deployment pipeline comparison view provide? (Choose two.)
options: A. Shows differences between content in adjacent stages.
B. Identifies items that are new, different, or only in one stage.
C. Automatically fixes all differences.
D. Deletes content from the source stage.
questions.explanation: The comparison view shows differences between content in adjacent stages (Development vs Test, Test vs Production) and identifies which items are new, different, or exist only in one stage. It does not automatically fix differences or delete source content.

questions.questionText: What is the benefit of configuring incremental refresh for a Power BI dataset?
options: A. It refreshes all data every time.
B. It only refreshes new and changed data based on date parameters, reducing refresh time and source system load.
C. It eliminates the need for a data gateway.
D. It converts the dataset to DirectQuery mode.
questions.explanation: Incremental refresh only refreshes data within a defined rolling window of new and changed data, significantly reducing refresh duration and load on source systems. It does not refresh all data, eliminate gateways, or convert to DirectQuery.
